title: How to Query Time Tracking Filtered By a Specific Time (Day)

posts.author: lizarazomiller (Miller Lizarazo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
I’m trying to Query my Team’s Time Tracking but I want to get specifically wich day and how many hours were added to their tasks.
I’ve already read all the API v2 Info but, there’s nothing related to a timestamp while querying the TimeTracking column , the data that I get from that column is “startDate” or sometimes “changed_at” but that doest work to me because that doesnt tell me how many hours my teammates spent on their tasks during the day.
Is there any way to query this? I think that it might be possible because monday allow me to filter by day-week-month-year the TimeTrack of my Teammates

Thank you

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @lizarazomiller – thanks for posting!

While you can query the summarized data from the time tracking column, at the moment there isn’t a way to use the API to drill into the sessions or start/end dates inside the column.

That said, I’ve gone ahead and added the “feature-request” tag to this topic and added it to our internal requests backlog so our product team knows it’s important to you!

posts.author: lizarazomiller (Miller Lizarazo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @dipro thank you so much for answering and for add the topic to the internal requests,
I got a last question, is it possible that in the future we could query the status column chages, for example I want to know when (date) the item started as pending then when it changed to working on it and finally when it changed to Done, it is simmilar to a timetracking log but aplied to the status colum
Thank you

posts.author: Dziugas (Džiugas Kazakevičius)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, is there any update on this feature?

posts.author: alessandra (Alessandra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Dziugas,

We currently do not support this feature but I’m happy to let our developers know of your interest.

What information are you looking to get specifically? What are you ultimately trying to do with the API?

Let me know! Maybe your use case is supported by other parts of our platform.

posts.author: Dziugas (Džiugas Kazakevičius)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for the reply @alessandra,

We use the time tracking feature and it would be nice to have an option to see time tracked in a chosen time frame. Right now, with the time tracking widget we can only track a day/week/month, we would want to track time from Jan 11th to 25th, for example, this is currently not possible.
It would also be nice to be able to visualize time tracked, when trying to use time tracking in the charts, it ignores which user tracked time and just uses the total column value instead, would be nice to be able to filter by user and date there as well.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello again @Dziugas,

Is this about a dashboard widget? Or are you trying to use the API to do this?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for that clarification @Dziugas,

Would you be able to please ask about this in our general FAQs section?

This section is related to the apps framework and the API.

In the other section there will be people more familiar with the widget and probably with an answer to your question

Cheers,
Matias
